items.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away

children.content: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

items.title: NT records two deaths from rare mosquito-borne disease

children.content: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A female Aedes aegypti mosquito bites someone's skin.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

gallery.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

thumbnail.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

items.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated

children.content: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.

image.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

image.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

items.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets

children.content: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

gallery.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Beams of sunlight and shadows from clouds fill the pink and blue sky.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

thumbnail.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Beams of sunlight and shadows from clouds fill the pink and blue sky.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

items.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?

children.content: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?

image.altText: Thermometers on a table with a healthcare worker in the background.

items.title: Putin lands in China for meeting with 'dear friend' Xi

children.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin smiles as he greets Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi at the airport in Beijing.

image.altText: Two men shake hands with several other people and an airplane behind them.

items.title: Arsenal ends 22-year Premier League drought after Man City draw

children.content: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

gallery.altText: Mikel Arteta and Leandro Trossard celebrate together while the Arsenal squad convene behind them

thumbnail.altText: Mikel Arteta and Leandro Trossard celebrate together while the Arsenal squad convene behind them

items.title: ASX to fall as inflation worries lead to US government debt sell-off

children.content: The Australian share market is likely to fall in early trade after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it. Follow live.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

items.title: Man dead, four injured in south-west Sydney shooting

children.content: A man has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

image.altText: Forensic officers load evidence bags into a van parked on a street.

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

items.title: 'Absolute madness': How a costume rock duo are breaking the internet

children.content: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

items.title: The men offering an alternative to 'manosphere' influencers

children.content: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

gallery.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.
A man in his late 20s with dark brown hair, smiling, sitting in farming equipment

thumbnail.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.
A man in his late 20s with dark brown hair, smiling, sitting in farming equipment

items.title: Quiz: Got the Wednesday blues? Let's take a trip around the world

children.content: Can you name the city in this picture? And what is the Canadian specialty food "beaver tail"?

items.title: Australia is wasting $1 billion on car parks nobody uses, report finds

children.content: Australia is wasting more than $1 billion each year building off-street car parks that end up unused and unwanted, a Grattan report has found.

image.altText: A woman holding her toddler son's hand walk through a car parking garage that is about half empty.

items.title: Green shoots after rain gives SA farmers hope after years of drought

children.content: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".

gallery.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.
A brown cow leaning towards a brown and white dog in a paddock with cows around them.
A man with a green jumper holding onto the back of a yellow truck.

thumbnail.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.
A brown cow leaning towards a brown and white dog in a paddock with cows around them.
A man with a green jumper holding onto the back of a yellow truck.

items.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic

children.content: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

image.altText: Generic photos of a mental health facility.

items.title: The young women chasing 'pure adrenaline' in motorsport drifting

children.content: A former track photographer, Georgia Tillyer bought her car, lovingly-named Night Fury, and learnt how to drive manual to enter her first event at Sydney Motorsport Park.

image.altText: Motorsport enthusiast Georgia Tillyer sits on top of a car holding a helmet

items.title: Six decades on and this Victorian community is still seeking its new bridge

children.content: Businesses relying on heavy transport to move goods across the Victoria-NSW border near Swan Hill say they are growing frustrated at the continual delays in replacing a 130-year-old wooden bridge.

items.title: The battle for Tasmania's north has been won, but locals aren't convinced

children.content: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

image.altText: Two football players celebrate during a game.

items.title: Vanuatu suggests Australia and China are 'undermining' it

children.content: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.

image.altText: Two men in suits shaking hands

items.title: Son of Mango founder arrested over father’s 100m cliff fall

children.content: A family spokesperson confirmed Jonathan Andic was being questioned by investigators. His father, Isak Andic, died in December 2024.

gallery.altText: A man in navy top and navy coat, walks with a police officer, with his head down.
A man with grey hair, glasses, dark suit and red tie, smiles as he stands in front of a wall branded with the Mango logo.

thumbnail.altText: A man in navy top and navy coat, walks with a police officer, with his head down.
A man with grey hair, glasses, dark suit and red tie, smiles as he stands in front of a wall branded with the Mango logo.

items.title: UK police investigate sex abuse allegations linked to Epstein files

children.content: A British police force has said it is investigating two allegations of child sex abuse following information in documents relating to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

image.altText: Jeffery Epstein smirks while sitting at a table with other people.

items.title: San Diego mosque shooters met online, shared 'hatred'

children.content: Authorities have revealed the teenagers who killed three people at a San Diego mosque met online and shared a "broad hatred" toward different religions and races.

image.altText: Two women embrace outside the Islamic Center of San Diego, wearing head scarfs and standing near police tape.

items.title: ARIA winner TISM causes $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House

children.content: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

gallery.altText: Seven men in balaclavas and black suits pose at the Opera House balcony overlooking the Harbour Bridge at Sunset

thumbnail.altText: Seven men in balaclavas and black suits pose at the Opera House balcony overlooking the Harbour Bridge at Sunset
